methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series D → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.5x) to $200M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $700M - $6.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

private.name: Redwood Materials
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series D), post-money $6B+

data.narrative:
Natron Energy is a Series D climate and energy technology company that has raised $200M+ to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comparable universe of nine private frontier-tech companies in the Climate & Energy sector, supplemented by one public peer (Lanzatech, ticker LNZA), spanning comparable and adjacent development stages.

Two quantitative methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis uses total capital raised ($200M) as a valuation proxy and applies a 1.5x step-up multiplier typical for Series D advancement, yielding $300M. Peer-median analysis examined four same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables—Redwood Materials ($6.0B, Series D), KoBold Metals ($2.0B, Series C), Electric Hydrogen ($1.0B, Series C), and Amogy ($700M, Series C)—generating a median valuation of $1.5B with a range spanning $700M to $6.0B.

The triangulated analysis across both methods supports a valuation range of $255M (low) to $1.7B (high), with a median of $900M. The wide dispersion between stage-progression and peer-median results reflects material uncertainty around Natron's relative positioning within the peer set. The peer median is substantially elevated by Redwood Materials' $6.0B valuation; excluding that outlier would narrow the range materially. Key uncertainties include technology differentiation, path to commercialization maturity relative to peers, and capital efficiency relative to total raised.
